name: Rabies Vaccine (Active PEP)
description: An inactivated viral vaccine that stimulates active immune antibody production against the Rabies lyssavirus. Indispensable for Post-Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) following animal bite exposures in endemic outposts.
preparation: Intramuscular injection (standard Essen 4-dose regimen: Days 0, 3, 7, and 14-28). Do not inject into gluteal region due to lower antibody response; use deltoid area in children.
safetyAlert: Administer immediately following exposure. There is no clinical contraindication when exposed, as Rabies infection is 100% fatal once symptoms emerge.
interactions: Concurrent immunosuppressant therapy or antimalarials like chloroquine may diminish vaccine response. Monitor closely.

demographicCautions.title: PREGNANCY SAFETY PROFILE
demographicCautions.message: Extremely safe in pregnancy. High risk of mortality overrides any potential minor vaccine side effects. Administer immediately after bite exposure.
